title: Probing the limits of the semiclassical Einstein equation
authors: Gustavo Schranck Habermann, Daniel A. Turolla Vanzella
abstract: In the context of semiclassical gravity, the semiclassical Einstein equation is often invoked when backreaction of quantum matter/fields on the spacetime is at stake. It is expected to hold when quantum fluctuations are small. Yet, it is routinely used to justify the central role of the expectation value of the stress-energy tensor of quantum fields, whose fluctuations formally diverge. Here we propose a new way to probe the limits of this approximation by exploiting peculiar nonlinearities of gravity. As a proof of principle, we construct a controlled, analytically tractable setting where the incoherent mixture of weak-gravity states drives the system into a strong-gravity regime. By selecting a branch-degenerate observable, one can compare predictions of quantum and semiclassical gravity, potentially delimiting the validity of the latter.
summary: This paper proposes a method to test the limits of the semiclassical Einstein equation by using a superposition of boosted masses. It shows that the nonlinear nature of gravity can cause the semiclassical average to predict a strong-gravity regime that is absent in the individual quantum branches. This provides a way to potentially distinguish between quantum and semiclassical gravity using branch-degenerate observables.
main_problem: Investigating the validity and limits of the semiclassical Einstein equation, specifically whether the approximation holds when quantum fluctuations of the stress-energy tensor are large or when nonlinear gravity is involved.
main_result: The authors demonstrate that the semiclassical approximation can predict a strong-gravity, nonlinear regime even when individual branches of a quantum superposition are in a weak-field regime, due to the amplification of energy-momentum in the averaged stress-energy tensor.
method: The study uses an analytical approach with the exact Levi-Civita metric in Weyl canonical form, applying Lorentz boosts to compare the predictions of a quantum superposition of states versus a semiclassical average.
model_or_system: A toy model consisting of a cylinder with low proper linear density in a coherent superposition of two opposite relativistic boost velocities.
key_observables: The rate of change of the proper circumference with respect to the proper radius (dC/dr).
important_parameters: Dimensionless mass parameter (sigma), Lorentz factor (gamma), and the Tolman gravitational mass per unit length (Sigma).
main_assumptions: The use of a 'flat-interior' approximation for the quantum scenario, which may break down in the semiclassical regime as the gravitational field becomes non-perturbative.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the difference in gravitational content between the two scenarios.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of semiclassical gravity validity, defines a toy model of a boosted cylinder, performs analytical calculations for both quantum and semiclassical scenarios, and compares observable predictions to demonstrate the breakdown of the approximation.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: A particle with mass m (with GNm/ℓ≪1) in either 4-momentum state |pµ 1⟩[see (a)] or |pµ 2⟩[see (b)] — or any other momentum state |pµ⟩, with pµ belonging to the mass shell pµpµ = −m2 (dashed gray line) —, would engender only a weak gravitational field — according to any covariant criterion —, regardless how large the values of energy and 3- momentum are in a given frame. However, a system with 4-momentum ⟨pµ⟩= (pµ 1 +pµ 2)/2 = (γm, 0) [see (c)] can generate a strong gravitational field provided γ is large enough.
